title: ITC-Sense of Presence Inventory (ITC-SOPI)
description: 44-item cross-media presence questionnaire. Developed by Lessiter, Freeman, Keogh & Davidoff (2001). Designed to measure presence across a wide range of media types (TV, film, VR, games). Focuses entirely on the user's experience with the media — no questions about system hardware or content story elements.
citation: Lessiter, J., Freeman, J., Keogh, E., & Davidoff, J. (2001). A cross-media presence questionnaire: The ITC-Sense of Presence Inventory. Presence: Teleoperators and Virtual Environments, 10(3), 282-297.
response_format: 5-point scale (1 = Strongly Disagree to 5 = Strongly Agree)

Sense_of_Physical_Space.description: Sense of being physically located in the mediated environment; spatial presence component. Analogous to Place Illusion (Slater, 2009).
example_items: I had a sense of being in the scenes displayed.
It felt as though I was in the same space as the characters or objects.
I felt I could reach out and touch things in the environment.
The space I was in felt real to me.

Engagement.description: Affective engagement, enjoyment, and emotional involvement with the media content. Distinct from spatial presence; captures affinity for the experience.

Ecological_Validity_Naturalness.description: Perceived naturalness and realism of the mediated environment; coherence of the virtual world with real-world expectations.
example_items: Things in the environment moved naturally.
Objects in the environment seemed to have the right scale.

Negative_Effects.description: Discomfort, disorientation, and negative side effects of the VR experience (cybersickness, dizziness, eyestrain). Reverse-coded for presence scoring.

scoring.method: Mean score per subscale; Negative Effects subscale is reverse-coded
scoring.composite_score: Optional: mean of all four subscale scores after NEG reversal
scoring.higher_score_means: Greater sense of presence / engagement / naturalness; fewer negative effects
scoring.note: Negative Effects score is sometimes reported separately as a cybersickness index

intended_use: Cross-media — usable with any media type from passive TV viewing to immersive VR
construct_measured: Multi-dimensional presence including physical space, engagement, naturalness, and negative effects
theoretical_basis: Empirically derived via factor analysis; not tied to a single theoretical model
known_limitations: Does not address specific system properties
Engagement subscale may conflate presence with enjoyment/entertainment
distinguishing_features: Only major presence questionnaire with a Negative Effects subscale
Widely used in ISPR conference community
